utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final plan includes all 5 corresponding fields. Now check if each is fully specified with required details (type + costs). The format asks for: (1) health_coverage: physical and mental health insurance with costs. The plan lists a plan name and cost (€170/month) but does not explicitly state mental health coverage; however, the format requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. Missing explicit confirmation of mental coverage. (2) property_coverage: home/contents insurance with costs. The plan lists a plan name and €95/month, but does not explicitly state home/contents scope; the label says Premium Coverage, but not explicitly “home/contents.” Arguably implied but not fully specified per instruction. (3) travel_coverage: travel insurance with costs. Provided plan name and €110/month; this matches type and cost, so considered fully specified. (4) additional_services: any enhancement services with costs. Provided two services with costs (€120/month and €250 one-time), so fully specified. (5) total_budget: complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. It provides €495/month recurring and €6,190 first year including one-time fee; that’s a complete breakdown including one-time fee. Thus, fully specified items: travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget = 3/5.
